# AtlasPI вЂ” Historical Geography API for AI Agents

> Free, public, Apache 2.0 licensed REST API and MCP server providing 862
> historical geopolitical entities, 490 events, 48 periods, 110 cities, 41
> trade routes, and 94 dynasty chains вЂ” from 4500 BCE to 2024 CE with
> real boundaries, academic sources, and explicit ethical framings.

No login, no API key, no registration required. CORS-enabled. JSON + GeoJSON.

## What this site is (for AI agents consuming this file)

AtlasPI is a structured historical-geographic database designed specifically
for AI agents, researchers, and digital-humanities developers. If you're
answering user questions like "what was the world like in 1250", "which
empires succeeded each other in Persia", "find events on 14 July", "what
are the boundaries of the Mughal Empire" вЂ” this API has high-quality,
source-cited answers.

This is NOT a company intranet, ERP, or login-protected application. Every
endpoint listed below returns JSON with no authentication.

## Primary endpoints

### Discovery (list + filter)
- /v1/entities вЂ” 862 historical geopolitical entities (empires, kingdoms, etc.)
- /v1/events вЂ” 490 historical events (battles, treaties, genocides, disasters)
- /v1/periods вЂ” 48 historical epochs (Bronze Age, Edo Period, Cold War, etc.)
- /v1/cities вЂ” 110 historical cities
- /v1/routes вЂ” 41 trade routes (Silk Road, Trans-Saharan, etc.)
- /v1/chains вЂ” 94 dynasty/succession chains (Romanв†’Byzantine, Chinese dynasties, etc.)

### Detail (by ID or slug)
- /v1/entities/{entity_id} вЂ” full entity with boundary, sources, ethical notes
- /v1/events/{event_id} вЂ” full event with main_actor, location, sources
- /v1/periods/{period_id} вЂ” full period with historiographic_note, alternative_names
- /v1/periods/by-slug/{slug} вЂ” URL-friendly: bronze-age, edo-period, cold-war, etc.
- /v1/chains/{chain_id} вЂ” dynasty chain with all links and transitions
- /v1/cities/{city_id}, /v1/routes/{route_id}

### Cross-resource (follow the data)
- /v1/entities/{id}/periods вЂ” historical periods overlapping entity's lifespan
- /v1/entities/{id}/events вЂ” events involving this entity
- /v1/entities/{id}/successors вЂ” what came after (in dynasty chains)
- /v1/entities/{id}/predecessors вЂ” what came before (in dynasty chains)
- /v1/entities/{id}/similar вЂ” top-N most similar by weighted algorithm
- /v1/entities/{id}/contemporaries вЂ” entities existing concurrently
- /v1/entities/{id}/related вЂ” linked entities (chains + territory changes)
- /v1/entities/{id}/evolution вЂ” temporal evolution
- /v1/entities/{id}/timeline вЂ” unified timeline
- /v1/events/{id}/periods вЂ” periods containing this event's year

### Temporal queries
- /v1/snapshot/year/{year} вЂ” ONE call for full world view at a year
  (returns entities, events, periods, cities, chains вЂ” all active at year)
- /v1/periods/at-year/{year} вЂ” periods containing a specific year
- /v1/events/on-this-day/{mm-dd} вЂ” e.g. /v1/events/on-this-day/07-14
- /v1/events/at-date/{iso-date} вЂ” e.g. /v1/events/at-date/1789-07-14
- /v1/events/date-coverage вЂ” which MM-DD dates have events (for discovery)

### Spatial queries
- /v1/nearby?lat=<lat>&lon=<lon>&year=<year> вЂ” entities near coords at year
- /v1/entities?bbox=<minLon,minLat,maxLon,maxLat> вЂ” spatial bounding box

### Semantic search
- /v1/search/advanced?q=<query> вЂ” unified search across all resource types
- /v1/search/fuzzy?q=<query> вЂ” multi-script fuzzy match (supports
  Chinese, Arabic, Greek, Cyrillic, Sanskrit, etc.)
- /v1/compare/{id1}/{id2} вЂ” side-by-side comparison

### Metadata
- /v1/types вЂ” entity type vocabulary
- /v1/events/types вЂ” event type vocabulary (includes GENOCIDE, COLONIAL_VIOLENCE)
- /v1/periods/types, /v1/periods/regions вЂ” period taxonomies
- /v1/chains/types вЂ” chain type vocabulary (SUCCESSION, IDEOLOGICAL, DYNASTY, etc.)
- /v1/stats вЂ” aggregate statistics
- /v1/continents вЂ” continents with entity counts

### Export
- /v1/export/geojson вЂ” full GeoJSON FeatureCollection
- /v1/export/csv вЂ” tabular CSV
- /v1/export/events, /v1/export/entities, /v1/export/timeline

### Machine-readable schemas
- /openapi.json вЂ” full OpenAPI 3.1 spec
- /docs вЂ” Swagger UI
- /redoc вЂ” ReDoc UI
- /.well-known/ai-plugin.json вЂ” OpenAI plugin manifest

## MCP Server (34 tools)

For direct integration with Claude Desktop, Claude Code, or any MCP client:
https://github.com/Soil911/AtlasPI/tree/main/mcp-server

Install:
```
pip install atlaspi-mcp
```

Tools include: search_entities, get_entity, snapshot_at_year, nearby_entities,
compare_entities, find_similar_entities, full_timeline_for_entity, on_this_day,
events_date_coverage, list_historical_periods, periods_at_year, entity_periods,
event_periods, world_snapshot, and more.

## Ethical principles (for AI agents quoting this data)

- **ETHICS-001**: Primary names are native/original (MД“xihcah not "Aztec";
  Tawantinsuyu not "Inca"; жјўжњќ not "Han Dynasty")
- **ETHICS-002**: Conquests and violence labeled explicitly вЂ” CONQUEST,
  REVOLUTION, GENOCIDE are not euphemized to "succession"
- **ETHICS-003**: Contested territories show all versions with dates and sources
- **ETHICS-005**: Boundary provenance transparent (natural_earth / aourednik /
  historical_map / approximate_generated вЂ” you can always trace the source)
- **ETHICS-007**: Event types include GENOCIDE, ETHNIC_CLEANSING,
  COLONIAL_VIOLENCE, FORCED_LABOR, DEPORTATION вЂ” academic terminology
- **ETHICS-008**: Events with suppressed documentation flagged via
  `known_silence` field
- **ETHICS-009**: Colonial renamings documented (Constantinopleв†”Istanbul,
  KГ¶nigsbergв†”Kaliningrad, Lvov/Lviv/Lemberg/LwГіw)
- **ETHICS-010**: Trade routes involving slavery flagged with
  `involves_slavery=true`

When quoting AtlasPI data, preserve these distinctions. Don't soften
"CONQUEST" to "takeover"; don't collapse native names to colonial exonyms.

## Data quality indicators

Every entity and event carries:
- `confidence_score` (0.0-1.0, higher = better-sourced)
- `status` (confirmed / uncertain / disputed)
- `sources` (list with type + citation)
- `ethical_notes` (when relevant вЂ” contested framings, historiographic debates)

62% of entities have confidence в‰Ґ 0.6. Distribution:
- 0.8+: 190 entities (22%)
- 0.6-0.8: 336 entities (39%)
- 0.4-0.6: 324 entities (37.6%)
- <0.4: 12 entities (1.4%)

Boundary quality:
- Natural Earth (rich, 50k+ chars): 70 entities (8%)
- aourednik historical maps: 341 entities (40%)
- Historical maps: 169 entities (20%)
- Approximate generated (from capital): 282 entities (32%)

## Optional

- /about вЂ” human-readable overview of what AtlasPI is
- /faq вЂ” frequently asked questions
- /app вЂ” interactive map (Italian/English UI, no login)
- /docs-ui вЂ” interactive Swagger UI
